text: I think I was the only public bull calling $AEHR an AI data center play a year ago when I started building my position under $10.

$INTC is reportedly in talks with $GOOGL and $AMZN for advanced packaging services. 

Intel’s CFO recently said the company is close to packaging deals worth billions of dollars a year.

And the beauty of this is every number above is verified by a third-party platform.

In a world where performance claims are easier than ever to fake due to AI, independent verification is the only thing that actually means anything:

savvytrader.com/StockSavvySh…

$AEHR is now the next multi-bagger in the growth portfolio. 

The thesis for building the position early was that if AI data center capacity triples this decade, Aehr only needs to capture a piece of the validation and burn-in layer for the stock to rerate much higher.

$PL shipped three more Pelican satellites to Vandenberg ahead of a SpaceX rideshare launch, expanding the constellation and boosting revisit rates.

The satellites deliver 50 cm imagery and use $NVDA powered onboard AI with Gen 2 planned for 2026.

Tom Lee's $BMNR now holds ~4% of all Ethereum.

They added ~71K $ETH last week bringing their total to ~4.8M.

$CRWD just added another $500M to its buyback plan bringing total authorization to $1.5B.

That is a notable signal management sees a disconnect between the stock price and the long-term value of the business.

$ONDS subsidiary 4M Defense won a border demining tender with potential value above $50M.

It is another sign that Ondas is starting to win larger autonomous defense programs with room to scale well beyond the initial award.

$JPM CEO Jamie Dimon says Europe is on a bad path and risks a slow decline unless it cuts bureaucracy, reforms its economy and becomes more competitive.

His broader point is that America needs a stronger Europe and a major trade deal could be one way to push both economic + military renewal.

Traffic through the Strait of Hormuz just hit its highest level since the opening days of the war.

A total of 21 ships passed through over the weekend which is a sign that movement through the corridor is picking back up.

$ASML is reportedly supplying ~20 EUV tools for Samsung’s P5 expansion as Samsung gets its next major chip facility ready for production next year.

Samsung is pushing aggressively to expand advanced memory capacity with ASML’s tools sitting right at the center of that buildout.

Bill Ackman said one of the biggest edges in investing is being willing to underwrite a business 3-5 years out which is why he owns $AMZN, $META & $GOOGL.

Volatility is the price you pay for long-term compounding when the market gives you the chance to buy value at a discount.

📺 RELATIVE STRENGTH IS LEADING THE TAPE

With the broader market struggling around key moving averages, select groups are clearly outperforming. 

Optical names like $AAOI and $LITE, space stocks such as $LUNR, $PL, and $ASTS, and memory plays like $SNDK and $WDC are showing leadership. 

The key is to focus on stocks that turn green first and hold strength while indices lag. 

Learn how we're making money by leaning into these leaders, adding on weakness, and trimming into strength as conditions shift.

If you enjoyed this video, please ❤️like and 🔁retweet

📺 THIS MARKET IS “BUILDING”… BUT NOT READY TO BREAK OUT

Current price action suggests either an early-stage recovery (base-building) or a temporary bounce before another leg down.

We are building… but not confirmed going higher yet.

*

$SPY $645 level is critical:
– Holding above it → bullish case stays alive (potential continuation higher)
– Breaking below it → pattern likely fails, risk of new lows increases

This is the decision zone for the market.

*

There’s an inverted head-and-shoulders pattern forming (potential bullish reversal).

Two main scenarios:
– Bullish path: hold support, build structure, eventually move toward $668–$671 resistance;
– Bearish trap: rally into resistance, suck in buyers, then roll over to new lows.

This could be a setup — or a trap.

*

So, if you bought the dip, trim into strength, lock in gains, and don’t assume trend continuation yet.

Then, watch how the price behaves: does it hold and build? Or fail and fade?

*

“Build vs Fail” Framework:
The market right now is “building… not yet proven it can go.”

Two possible outcomes: 
– Build → Go (trend continuation)
– Build → Fail (fake breakout, reversal)

Right now, wait for confirmation.

*

I also looked at $XLE. 

Energy had a strong run, now it is showing signs of cooling, broke below the short-term trend (8-day MA), and is still above key support (21-day MA).

It's not bearish yet; likely entering a consolidation phase. While energy pauses, beaten-down sectors may bounce.

Look for optical $AAOI $LITE / memory names $SNDK $WDC and space stocks $ASTS $PL $LUNR. 

*

So, for today:
– For strength: $SPX should hold current levels and form higher lows;
– Weakness signal: losing those levels → structure breaks.

Best strategy right now remains: stay tactical, trim profits, wait for confirmation, and don’t chase.
